place: Roseland borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Roseland was originally the Centreville section of Livingston before becoming its own borough.
A resident named Bill Teed led the petition that established Roseland as a separate borough.
The borough’s recreation center features three baseball fields, tennis, volleyball, basketball courts, and an ice skating rink.
Every year Roseland hosts Shredding Days, a community event that encourages residents to recycle paper and other materials.
Residents can request shade trees to be planted in the right‑of‑way in front of their property through the town’s tree‑planting program.
The 2026 Easter Egg‑Stravaganza takes place on Saturday, March 28 from 10 a.m. to noon, drawing families to a local celebration.
